frozen_components: GPT-3, GPT-Neo, OPT language models (not fine-tuned, used in zero-shot or few-shot prompting)
architecture.summary: The paper evaluates and proposes methods for combining parametric knowledge (memorized in LM parameters) and non-parametric knowledge (retrieved text) for open-domain QA. The core system is a retrieval-augmented LM pipeline where a retrieval module (BM25, Contriever, or GenRead) fetches relevant passages from Wikipedia, which are concatenated with the input question before being fed to a decoder-only LM (GPT-3, GPT-Neo, OPT). The 'Adaptive Retrieval' method adds a popularity-based gate that decides whether to invoke retrieval at all, based on the subject entity's Wikipedia page-view count and relationship type, allowing the LM to rely on parametric memory when reliable and retrieval when necessary.
trained_components: Contriever dense retriever (pretrained and fine-tuned on MS MARCO)

biblio.title: When Not to Trust Language Models: Investigating Effectiveness of Parametric and Non-Parametric Memories

claims.statement: LMs struggle with less popular factual knowledge; scaling fails to appreciably improve memorization of factual knowledge in the long tail. For the 4,000 least popular questions in PopQA, GPT-j 6B has 16% accuracy and GPT-3 davinci-003 has 19% accuracy.

Subject entity popularity has a strong positive correlation with model accuracy across relationship types. GPT-3 davinci-003 shows the highest positive correlation (roughly 0.4) while GPT-Neo-1.3B shows relatively weak positive correlations (approximately 0.1).

Retrieval-augmented LMs significantly outperform unassisted LMs. A much smaller LM (GPT-Neo 2.7B) augmented by Contriever retrieval outperforms vanilla GPT-3 davinci-003. Contriever gives 7% accuracy gains on top of GPT-3 davinci-003.

Retrieval-augmentation can hurt performance on questions about popular entities. For 10% of questions, retrieval causes the LM to incorrectly answer a question it could otherwise answer correctly, with significantly lower recall@1 (0.14 vs 0.42 overall).

Adaptive Retrieval improves performance over non-adaptive methods. The best performance on PopQA using GPT-3 davinci-003 adaptively with GenRead and Contriever yields 46.5% accuracy, 5.3% higher than any non-adaptive method.

Adaptive Retrieval reduces inference latency. For GPT-J 6B, inference time latency almost doubles with concatenated retrieved context; Adaptive Retrieval enables reducing inference time up to 9% from standard retrieval. Adaptive Retrieval also reduces GPT-3 API costs by half.
On EntityQuestions, Adaptive Retrieval reduces API costs by 15% while maintaining equivalent performance to retrieval-only.

The paper emphasizes that its findings are limited to entity-centric factual knowledge evaluated on synthetic datasets (PopQA and EntityQuestions), and the extent to which results apply to naturally occurring factual knowledge has not been firmly established. It is an open question if the same findings are applicable to other types of world knowledge such as commonsense.
